qa.user_verbatim_complaint: /allow 拦不住 tracker-gate high small 你给的 `find_red_cells.py > docs/research/case-3` 命令被拦——先复现确定是哪个 hook
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: pretool-tool-policy.py now exits 0 for ba subagent redirecting to /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3 (M1: */docs/research/ added to ba.allowed_write_path_prefixes). pretool-bash-safety.sh sentinel block now consulted for IS_SUBAGENT=1 (M2: IS_SUBAGENT guard removed at former line 483). Task ID derivation aligned between writer and reader (M3). All 7 pytest ACs pass (7 passed in 1.73s).

complaint_element_pointer.page_url: N/A — hook infrastructure, no browser UI

qa.overall_assessment: PASS. All three primary blockers (M1 policy prefix, M2 IS_SUBAGENT guard, M3 task ID alignment) are correctly fixed. All 7 pytest acceptance criteria pass. All structural invariants (daemon-restart prohibition Layers 1.A-1.E, CF-1 comment-only attack invariant, IS_SUBAGENT legacy firewall at line 145, subagent write firewall in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) remain intact. Two Codex-flagged BLOCKERs (posttool sentinel consumption for subagents; posttool task_id alignment) are explicitly excluded from this cycle per context JSON scope.excluded and BA spec W3 (Won't Have: No changes to PostToolUse sentinel consumption logic). Dev report's recommendations section explicitly surfaces both as SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED items. This is the correct treatment per Anti-Fraud Principle 7 (cycle scope vs original bug scope). settings.json Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) entry is a legitimate additive improvement supporting the user's use case.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex BLOCKER #1 (session 019e5984-57d7-7a83-ba6e-a35efe850bd4): M2 now lets ba subagents through sentinel check in pretool-bash-safety.sh, but posttool-allowlist-consume.py still exits early for subagents (is_subagent_context check at line 76). Sentinel grants matched by subagents are not consumed and remain reusable until expiry/stop cleanup, violating single-use /allow semantics. Explicitly excluded from this cycle by BA spec W3 and context JSON scope.excluded. Dev report recommends SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED.

Codex BLOCKER #2 (session 019e5984-57d7-7a83-ba6e-a35efe850bd4): posttool-allowlist-consume.py task_id derivation still prefers payload task_id field before session_id, mirroring the pre-M3 mismatch. In the scenario where task_id != session_id, pretool grants SENTINEL_OK using session_id key but posttool cannot find and unlink that file. Explicitly excluded from this cycle per W3. Dev report recommends SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED.

Codex MAJOR #3: M1 uses */docs/research/ (broader than BA spec Codex-recommended */docs/research/case-). Context JSON development_approach.strategy explicitly offered both options as valid ('*/docs/research/case- per Codex recommendation, or */docs/research/ for broader research output'). Dev chose the broader option. User's actual command uses docs/research/ as base directory. The broader prefix correctly unblocks the user's stated use case and matches the spirit of the user requirement. QA classifies this as out-of-scope observation; the broader option was explicitly authorized in the strategy field.

Codex MAJOR #4: Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) in settings.json allow list is a broad permanent permission. Context JSON dispatch prompt explicitly requires 'settings.json: Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) present in allow list' as a verification criterion. Dev added this per acceptance criterion. Codex finding is out-of-scope for this cycle — the criterion was explicitly required.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex session 019e5984-57d7-7a83-ba6e-a35efe850bd4 raised 2 BLOCKERs, 3 MAJORs, 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. BLOCKER #1: subagent sentinels not consumed — posttool-allowlist-consume.py exits early for subagents. BLOCKER #2: M3 fix not applied to posttool task_id derivation. MAJOR #3: M1 uses */docs/research/ (broader than recommended case- prefix). MAJOR #4: settings.json Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*) is broad. MAJOR #5: AC1 full-hook-chain claim under-verified (only 2 of N hooks tested). OBSERVATION_ONLY #6: comments mention removed --regex syntax.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: BLOCKER #1 and #2: Classified out_of_scope. Both posttool-allowlist-consume.py gaps were explicitly excluded from this cycle by BA spec W3 ('No changes to PostToolUse sentinel consumption logic') and context JSON scope.excluded. Dev report already identifies them as SEPARATE CYCLE NEEDED in recommendations. They are surfaced in out_of_scope_observations with security_relevant=true. Not verdict-blocking for this cycle. MAJOR #3: Out_of_scope — context JSON strategy field explicitly listed both options. MAJOR #4: Out_of_scope — dispatch prompt explicitly requires the settings.json entry. MAJOR #5: Out_of_scope — AC1 test is correctly scoped to the two identified blocking hooks (pretool-bash-safety.sh + pretool-tool-policy.py). BA spec confirms these are the two blocking hooks; testing all 13 hooks is beyond cycle scope. OBSERVATION_ONLY #6: Logged.

summary.cycle_scope_note: Cycle scope PASS. Two out-of-scope gaps in posttool-allowlist-consume.py (sentinel not consumed for subagents; task_id derivation mismatch in posttool) exist and are flagged in out_of_scope_observations as security_relevant=true. These are W3 Won't Have per BA spec and explicitly recommended for a follow-up cycle in dev report.
